steps.title: Apply cofunction identities and substitute y = 0
steps.content:
Recognize that $\cos(90 - a)° = \sin a°$ and $\sin(90 - b)° = \cos b°$ (cofunction identities). When $y = 0$, $\sin(a + y)° = \sin a°$.

Substitute these into the expression:
$$2\sin a° \cdot \cos b° + \sin a° \cdot \cos b°$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
Both terms contain $\sin a° \cdot \cos b°$, so factor and add:
$$\sin a° \cdot \cos b° (2 + 1) = 3\sin a° \cdot \cos b°$$

steps.title: Substitute given values and evaluate
steps.content:
Now substitute $\sin a° = 0.50$ and $\cos b° = 0.99$:
$$3 \cdot 0.50 \cdot 0.99$$

Calculate step by step: $3 \cdot 0.50 = 1.5$, then $1.5 \cdot 0.99 = 1.485$.

The value of the expression is 1.485.
